KIRA_CH1_THORNWICK.description:
Rain comes down in sheets, soaking through a cloak too thin to matter. Behind you the cottage burns, eight years of safety collapsing into smoking timber and orange light across every puddle.

You don't look back. Looking back is how they catch you; that lesson cost more than it should have.

Your satchel holds what's left of a life: three books of void magic in your mother's hand, a locket with her face worn soft from thumbing it too often, a map of Crownhaven memorized before you understood why. Somewhere under the old palace, in catacombs your parents died keeping secret, a crown piece waits.

The rain tastes like ash. You've stopped being surprised by that.

KIRA_CH1_KINGS_ROAD.description:
The King's Road unrolls ahead, mud churned soft by centuries of carts and cavalry. You walk it in disguise, a peasant girl with dirt smeared just so and calluses faked with a rock last night. Eight years hiding your nature, and the void still sits coiled in your chest like something that never quite sleeps.

Near Millbrook you smell the caravan before you see it: horse sweat, wet rope, the copper tang of fear that clings no matter how it's dressed.

Three bandits have a merchant wagon cornered against the ditch. The merchant is pleading, something about his daughter, his wife.

The void doesn't ask permission. It leans forward, patient as a held knife, waiting to see what you'll let it do.

KIRA_CH1_MARCHES.description:
The Ashen Marches stretch ahead, where the old world finished dying and never got buried. Ash crunches underfoot, the air tasting of sulfur and something like regret, thick enough to coat your throat.

Things move at the edge of sight: half-formed, twisted, born from whatever the Sundering broke open. Their eyes hold no light at all.

The Watch guards this border, mercenaries paid to kill what crawls from the Waste. They could help you. They could just as easily decide you're worth killing too.

Somewhere in the dark, a child is crying, the kind that's given up expecting anyone to come.

The void pulses low in your veins, patient and certain you'll use it before the night's out.

choices.text: Use magic to save the child

KIRA_CH1_CIRCLE.description:
The Circle's tower hides in plain sight. Ask three people and you'll get three buildings; only you see the truth, stone black as a starless sky. The stairs spiral longer than the tower should hold.

At the top waits the Archmage: ageless, silver-haired, eyes reading you from ten years either direction.

'We know who you are, Kira Nightwind,' she says, the name landing like a held breath let go. 'We felt your void magic from across the kingdom.'

She knows about the crown, your parents, the Cult hunting you since the fire.

'We can help you.' A pause, precise as a knife laid flat. 'But the price is service.'

You catalogue the exits before answering. Old habit. She doesn't mind.

KIRA_CH1_BANDITS.description:
Three bandits. One wagon. A family pleading for their lives, voices ragged.

The void surges up your arms like a tide finding a lower shore, and for once you don't fight it. Eight years hiding this, eating scraps, playing nothing. Not tonight.

Cold floods your fingertips first, white to the knuckle. The first bandit drops before his mind catches up. The second bolts; darkness closes around his throat like a fist you're not sure is still yours to open.

The third kneels, breath fogging in the cold you've made. 'Mercy,' he chokes. 'We're with the Cult—'

He doesn't finish. You already knew. The Cult is closer than the ash on this road, and you just told them where to find you.

KIRA_CH1_CHILD.description:
The boy can't be older than six, hunger carving his face too old for it. Hollow eyes, cracked lips, a stillness no child should learn young.

'Don't hurt me,' he whispers.

You reach for the void anyway, not to strike but to shield. The cold still comes, needling your fingertips even turned soft. Shadow wraps him like a blanket that breathes. The things hunting the Waste flinch and lose interest.

The boy isn't afraid of you. He's looking at you like an answer he's been too scared to ask for.

'You're like me,' he says. 'The shadows listen to you.'

Under his ribs, faint as a heartbeat through a wall, you feel it: the void, small and real. Not an orphan. One of yours.

KIRA_CH1_WATCH.description: The Watch is everything the kingdoms pretend not to know about: a wall of steel and sacrifice holding the line, smelling of wet wool and pitch smoke before you're even through the gate. Their commander is a scarred woman named Voss, and she reads you the way you read strangers, sizing up angles before deciding you're not a threat. 'A Nightwind,' she says. 'We wondered when you'd show.' She knows your family, your legacy, the weight you've hauled since Millbrook burned. 'The Cult is moving. All seven pieces. They mean to reunite them at the Temple of the Void, and when they do—' She lets it die there. She doesn't need to finish it.

KIRA_CH1_WASTELAND.description: Three days through the wasteland and the ash never stops falling, fine and grey, tasting of rust when it lands on your tongue. The sky has stayed the same dead color the whole way, as if the sun died with the Void Kingdom and nobody told the clouds. On the second night you find a giant's skeleton, bones black as coal. Touch one and the void pulses back like a second heartbeat under your palm. Seated in the ribcage, a piece of the crown, calling to you the way your own name would. The first piece. Just waiting. You didn't expect it to be this easy, which is exactly why you don't trust it.

KIRA_CH1_OFFER.description:
The offer is simple: five years of service, and the Circle helps you burn the Cult down. But you've spent eight years reading faces for the lie underneath the words, and hers has one. They don't want the crown destroyed. They want it leashed, and you're the leash.

'Your parents made the same choice,' the Archmage says, watching you the way you watch exits. 'They served us. Then betrayed us. Look where it got them.'

Your parents served the Circle. The thought sits wrong, a fact you can't put back.

'Accept our help,' she presses, 'or walk away. But know this: the Cult already knows you're alive, and they have more mages than you can imagine.'

choices.text: Reject them—you'll find your own way

KIRA_CH1_TRUTH.description: You sit across from the Archmage and you make yourself still, the way you learned to be still when the Cult searched Millbrook house by house. He tells you everything, and the room smells of hot wax and old paper, candle smoke thinning as he talks like the flames themselves are listening. The crown was Aethon's power, he says. Seven pieces, each holding a fragment of the first silence. United, they could unmake reality itself. Your parents were guardians, chosen to protect one piece. When the Cult came, they hid it the only place the Cult couldn't search: inside you. In your soul. 'That's why they can find you,' he says. 'The piece calls to its brothers. The Cult hears its voice.' You have carried a piece of the crown since before you could walk. It has been screaming your location into the dark for seventeen years, and you never once heard it.

KIRA_CH1_INTERROGATE.description:
You keep one bandit breathing, just enough to talk. It doesn't take much; a hand of shadow around a throat loosens with promises, tightens with lies, and he knows the difference.

The Cult has hunted Nightwinds for generations, he gasps. Your family was the last standing. Eight years ago they caught your parents and somehow missed you, a gap he clearly still doesn't understand.

'Vespera will want to know you're alive,' he wheezes. 'She'll send the Hollow Knights. The seven pieces have to be united at the Temple—'

He dies mid-sentence. Vespera. The Temple. Seven pieces.

This was never just revenge. You've been walking toward the end of the world and calling it grief.

KIRA_CH1_AFTERMATH.description:
The merchant can't stop thanking you, voice cracking on every word. He offers a ride to Crownhaven, then something worth more: information.

'I traded with the Cult once,' he admits, 'until I saw what they did to children they took. They want the crown whole again, the Void Kingdom back. Something worse waits behind that, and even they're afraid of it.'

He presses a silver crescent into your hand, cold enough to sting. 'The Circle of Mages. Find them. They'll help you when the world won't.'

You take the road to Crownhaven, the crescent a small weight in your pocket, the void a larger one behind your ribs. People keep offering help lately. You've learned to price it before you say yes.

KIRA_CH1_ORPHAN.description:
The Circle takes the boy in without hesitation. They see what you see: a void kindling under his ribs, another blade to point at the Cult someday. Then the Archmage looks at you.

'He has talent,' she says. 'So did your parents. We know how that ended.'

So you're the one who teaches him. Not what you wanted, but wanting was never on the table.

His name is Tom. Gap-toothed, six going on exhausted, humming a tuneless scrap of song whenever he's scared, which is often. Afraid of the dark and fascinated by it in the same breath, the way you were once.

Three months pass. When you leave for Crownhaven, he cries without hiding it, and you realize you've started something that might outlast you.

KIRA_CH1_PARENTS.description:
Commander Voss doesn't soften it. Your parents were Kira and Marcus Nightwind, two of the strongest void mages of their generation, sworn to guard crown pieces for forty years before you were born. You were named for her. Nobody told you that until now, and it lands somewhere tender you didn't know you still had.

'The Cult caught them in your village,' Voss says. 'Your mother fought. Your father died putting his body between you and them. Your mother—'

She stops.

'Your mother struck a deal. She gave herself to the Hollow King so you'd live. That's the only reason you're breathing. It's also why the Cult never stopped hunting you. You're the key to getting her back.'

Your mother isn't dead. She's something worse than dead, held somewhere you can't reach by something that doesn't let go of what it takes.

You try to remember her voice and get almost nothing back: a hum in a dark room, three notes of a lullaby that never finished, the way she used to say your name like a small precious secret. Eight years old, and grief is already practiced at hiding the good parts first.

choices.text: It's too late—she'd want you to survive
Hunt down the Hollow King yourself

KIRA_CH1_CROWN_FIRST.description:
The crown piece is colder than any crown has a right to be, and the moment your palm closes on it the world goes silent, sound arriving late like it's traveling from somewhere very far away.

Visions flood in without asking. Aethon the Undying, kneeling before something too vast to be called a face. The Void Beast. A god built to eat other gods. Aethon shattered his own crown rather than let it fall whole into that hunger, seven pieces scattered across centuries, and buried in the vision is a face you've half seen before, in your father's old portraits. Nightwind. Someone in your blood carried this weight and didn't manage to put it down clean either.

The pieces call to each other like something singing underwater. The Cult already holds six. This is the seventh.

When the visions let go, your fingertips have gone bone-white and stay that way, frost etched into the first knuckle deep enough it won't melt back out. First scars. Something tells you they won't be the last.

You carry two pieces of the crown now. Somewhere out there, the Beast has learned your face.

choices.text: Use the power to find the others
Destroy this piece—before it's too late

KIRA_CH2_CROWNHAVEN.description: Crownhaven rises out of the fog ahead of you, a city of a million souls built on the bones of the old world, wet stone slick under your boots and tallow smoke hanging low over the rooftops. Somewhere a bell tower rings the hour a beat late, then another answers it even later, like the whole city has forgotten how to keep time since the world started dying. The old imperial palace looms over everything, its catacombs a maze of secrets nobody living remembers burying. You have one goal: the crown piece hidden somewhere inside those walls. Your parents died protecting its location, and now you're the one meant to finish what that cost them. The palace is guarded, but you know a way past the guards, drawn in your mother's own hand in the spellbook she left behind. Below, in the dark under the city, something is already waiting for you to arrive.

choices.text: Posh as a noble—walk right in

KIRA_CH2_CATACOMBS.description: The catacombs drop for miles under the city, the air going colder, thick with wet limestone and something underneath it you don't want to name. Void script covers the walls: warnings, prophecies, names of the dead. You read them as you pass, unable to stop. Your great-grandmother. Your grandfather. Every Nightwind who walked this corridor and didn't walk back out. At the bottom, the chamber: seven pedestals, six empty, one holding a fragment of the crown. A Hollow Knight guards it, eyes like burned-out lanterns, a sword that swallows light. 'Kira Nightwind,' it grinds out. 'The Master expects you.' You count the exits anyway. Old habit. Might be the only reason you're still breathing.

KIRA_CH2_BATTLE.description: The Hollow Knight moves faster than anything human should, its sword arriving in your mind before it arrives at your body: strike, parry, counter, no gap between thought and steel. You're faster. The void answers when you call it this time, no hesitation, shadow tearing off the chamber walls to wrap the Knight's limbs like rope. You pour eight years into it, eight years of hiding under floorboards, eight years of being afraid of your own hands, eight years of rage you never had anywhere safe to put. Frost climbs your knuckles as the power drains through you, the old scars on your fingertips gone bone-white and aching. The Knight screams, a sound like a wagon axle tearing loose, and comes apart into darkness. You're alone in the chamber, breathing like you ran here, and the crown piece on the floor pulses with a warmth that doesn't belong to anything you understand. Etched into the stone beside it, in void light already fading, your mother's hand: Forgive me. Find the truth. And when you're ready, destroy it all.

KIRA_CH2_NEGOTIATE.description: 'The Master wants you alive,' the Hollow Knight says, voice grinding like stone dragged over stone. 'The others, less so.' You keep your voice level, the way you train yourself to when the alternative is letting them see your pulse. 'Who is your Master? What does he want?' 'The Hollow King. He wants the crown. And he wants you specifically. Your bloodline made the seal. Your bloodline can break it.' It steps aside. Not defeated. Permitting. 'Take the piece, Kira Nightwind. Bring it to the Temple. Meet your mother there.' Something goes cold in your stomach that has nothing to do with the void. Your mother is alive. The only question left is what that costs you.

choices.text: Attack while its guard is down

KIRA_CH2_BYPASS.description: You let go and become shadow, cold enough that the air goes silent before you move. The Knight passes through the space where you were and senses nothing. The crown piece waits unobstructed, black metal that drinks torchlight. You reach for it and the visions take you whether you're ready or not. Aethon the Undying, screaming as the Void Beast tears his soul apart. His last act, shattering the crown across time: seven fragments, seven guardians, seven sacrifices spent before you were born. Then her, your mother, caged in void light, screaming a name it takes a second to place as your own. The vision drops you. The piece is in your hand, fingers numb, and behind you the Hollow Knight turns.

choices.text: Use the piece as a weapon

KIRA_CH2_CROWN.description: The crown piece sits warm in your palm, warmer than metal has any right to be, almost breathing. It whispers in the grammar of the void, which isn't words so much as absence shaped like words. Through it you can feel the other six, scattered across the kingdoms like a wound you can suddenly map. The Cult holds five. The Circle holds one. You are holding the seventh, and your fingertips have already started going numb around the edges where the frost always starts. In your mind a map unfolds on its own: the Temple of the Void, buried deep in the Ashen Wastes, where every path you could walk from here eventually leads. The final convergence. The reunification. Your mother will be there. The Hollow King will be there. Whatever the world becomes after this gets decided in that room, not out here. Three roads stretch out in front of you, and none of them lead back to the girl who ran from the fire in Millbrook.

choices.text: Study the piece more—understand the power
Follow the truth to its end

KIRA_CH2_ESCAPE.description: You run, the crown piece burning cold against your hip, the Hollow Knight's screams still bouncing off stone behind you. You break into night air gasping, and Crownhaven spreads out below: a million lights, not one of them aware of what's coming. The piece pulses, counting off six more fragments drawing closer. The Cult knows you have this one now. They'll send everything. Your mother's face surfaces, not the way she is now, caged in the void, but the way she was: laughing, guiding your hand through a spell, holding you when the dark outside the cottage window was only ever just dark. You're not letting her down twice.

choices.text: Find the Circle—unite against the Cult

KIRA_CH3_TEMPLE.description:
The Temple of the Void rises out of the Ashen Wastes like something the land grew instead of something anyone built: a tower of black stone your eyes slide off if you look at it too directly, reality bending at its edges the way heat bends air over a fire. It smells of scorched dust and something colder underneath, something with no smell a body should recognize.

Six Cultists ring the dais, each holding a crown piece, each wearing a silver mask that catches no light at all. And at the center, suspended in a cage woven from pure void light, hangs your mother.

'Kira.' Her voice makes it across the distance as barely more than breath. 'You shouldn't have come.'

The seventh piece warms in your grip and starts to glow, a low answering pulse. The six in the Cultists' hands flare back in response, six points of cold light closing a circuit you didn't ask to complete. Somewhere in the wrongness of this place, you feel him. The Hollow King. He's been waiting for you since before you knew his name, and the frost climbing your fingers tells you he already knows you're here.

choices.text: Use the piece to communicate with your mother
Offer yourself in exchange for her freedom

KIRA_CH3_CHARGE.description:
You go in low and fast, void blazing off both hands in ribbons that eat the torchlight whole. The first three Cultists fall before they finish turning, shadow torn apart by a deeper shadow. There are more behind them. There are always more.

Then the air changes. The Hollow King manifests out of the space between the fallen, formless, hungry, and it wraps around your ribs and squeezes the breath out of your lungs like it's testing how much you can lose before you stop being useful.

'Your bloodline made me,' it hisses, close enough that you feel it more than hear it. 'Your great-great-grandfather bound me here. Now you'll undo it.'

Your mother screams somewhere behind the dark. The crown pieces tear loose from six Cultist hands and spiral toward you, seven fragments straining to become one, your hand already lifting to meet them before you've decided to let it.

You understand the shape of the choice now, plain as a blade: become the Undying, or die finding out what happens if you refuse. Frost has climbed past your wrist and you can't feel your fingers anymore.

choices.text: Free your mother instead—let the crown be

KIRA_CH3_TALK.description:
You push through the void connection the crown piece offers, past static and cold and your own pulse arriving a beat late, until you find something that answers back: your mother's mind, still there, still hers.

Eight years in this place have worn her down to something half shadow, tortured past what you let yourself imagine on the road here, but she's still fighting, and that alone nearly undoes you more than anything else has tonight.

'The crown is a trap,' she whispers, the words thin and effortful. 'Aethon didn't seal the Beast. He became it. The crown is its prison, and whoever wears it becomes the jailer.'

The Hollow King screams somewhere above you both, feeling the intrusion for what it is. Your mother's last act of will tears a visible hole through his concentration, a crack you feel more than see.

'Now,' she gasps, voice fraying. 'While he's weak. Finish it.'

The seven pieces lift and turn toward you, waiting on a decision you have maybe three heartbeats to make. Frost has crawled past your wrist while you weren't watching your own hand.

choices.text: Destroy the crown—and yourself with it

KIRA_CH3_BARGAIN.description:
'Take me,' you say, before you can talk yourself out of it. 'Let her go. I'm the last Nightwind. I'm what you actually want.'

The Hollow King goes still, a stillness with weight to it, like the room itself is holding its breath. Your mother's scream tears through the cage of void light. No, no, no, she says, and you make yourself not look at her, because if you look at her you won't be able to finish this.

'Kira, don't.'

'Very well.' The darkness peels off the walls and wraps around you instead, and it feels exactly like drowning in ice, cold going in through your skin and settling somewhere past it. 'Your mother will live. You will spend eternity in the void with me.'

It isn't your magic it takes, or your name. It's simpler than that and worse: the version of you that still got to choose. Your mother falls free of the cage as the dark pulls you down into it, and the last thing you see is her face, streaked and reaching, mouthing your name like it might still work as a rope.

Then there's only the void, and the hunger, and the frost has gone past your wrist.

KIRA_ENDING_MARTYR.description:
You destroy the crown. All seven pieces shatter at once, releasing the Void Beast—and you along with it.

In your final moment, you see the world freed. The Hollow King screams as he's unmade. Your mother feels peace for the first time in eight years.

And you? You become part of the void—not trapped, but free. A new guardian, watching over the world you saved.

The crown is gone. The Beast is gone. And in the silence of the void, you finally rest.

KIRA_ENDING_UNDYING.description:
You claim the crown. Power floods through you—unimaginable, terrifying, beautiful.

You are Aethon's heir now. The Undying. A god of the void.

The Hollow King cowers before you. You could destroy him—or you could use him.

Your mother looks at you with fear in her eyes. You've become something else. Something beyond human.

The world will never be the same. And neither will you.

KIRA_ENDING_HOLLOW.description:
You give yourself to the Hollow King. In exchange, your mother lives—a mortal again, free at last.

But you? You're lost. Part of the darkness forever. A new prisoner in the void.

Sometimes, in her dreams, your mother feels you there. Reaching. Wanting. Never quite gone.

And the crown remains, waiting for the next Nightwind to make the same choice.

KIRA_ENDING_VICTOR.description:
You claim the crown, but this time—differently. The seventh piece in your hand doesn't amplify the power. It balances it.

The void flows through you, not to consume, but to transform. The Cultists feel it and scream—their pieces shatter, releasing the souls they've taken. The Hollow King writhes, dissolving into the nothing it came from.

And your mother? She falls free, mortal again, weeping with joy.

The crown crumbles to dust around you. Not destroyed—redeemed. The void no longer hungers. It serves.

You are not Undying. You are not Martyr. You are Kira Nightwind, who chose a third path: victory for everyone.

KIRA_ENDING_SURVIVOR.description:
You don't want the crown. You never did.

In the final moment, you use the seventh piece not to claim power, but to tear a hole in reality itself. The void opens—and you grab your mother, diving through.

Behind you, the Temple collapses. The Cult screams as the void consumes them. The Hollow King howls with rage, trapped in collapsing dimensions.

And you? You emerge in a field you've never seen, under stars you've never known. Your mother is alive. Mortal. Breathing.

The crown is gone. The Cult thinks you're dead. In this new world, you're just two women starting over.

Sometimes you feel the void still calling. But you have each other. And that's enough.

KIRA_ENDING_LOST.description:
You try to become the bridge—to hold both the void and the light. But the crown was never meant for balance.

Reality tears. The world screams—and you're pulled into the space between.

You see everything now: every Nightwind who came before, every choice that led here. Your mother and father, reaching for you. The Hollow King, laughing. The Beast, waiting.

You are not destroyed. Not transformed. Lost—suspended in the between-place, connected to all things and none.

Sometimes, in dreams, you reach through to the living world. Sometimes, those who listen hear your whispers.

You are no longer Kira. But you are not gone either. You are the bridge. And bridges, eventually, get used.

KIRA_CH1_STUDY.description:
You force yourself to sit still with the crown piece instead of running from what it shows you. Better to know the shape of the wound than flinch every time it aches.

The visions come easier the second time, though no less terrible: the Void Beast's hunger, Aethon's desperation as he broke the one thing that might have saved him rather than let it fall whole into something worse. Every vision has a price stitched into it, paid in advance by someone who isn't you yet.

Your fingertips stay cold long after you set the piece down, a reminder that curiosity here isn't free. Knowledge might be power, but out here power is also just another way to bleed.

KIRA_CH2_PREPARE.description: You spread the Circle's reports across a table still tacky with spilled tallow, and force yourself to read every line twice before you move. Six crown pieces accounted for, all in Vespera's keeping. The seventh sits somewhere inside palace walls you haven't mapped yet. You trace the routes with a fingertip, the frostbite scar at your knuckle catching on the parchment's edge, a small cold reminder of what the void expects in return. You count what you can use instead of what you feel: six pieces, one enemy, one clock running down to nothing. Crownhaven doesn't wait for anyone to be ready.

KIRA_CH3_ALLIES.description: Word travels faster than you expected once you stop trying to do this alone. The Circle sends mages you've spent a year distrusting. The City Watch sends soldiers who'd have arrested you on sight a month ago. Stranger still, two former Cultists show up asking for nothing but a chance to undo something, their too-clean robes traded for plain wool that still smells faintly of incense they can't wash out. You count heads instead of history, because history is a luxury you can't afford tonight. It isn't an army. It might, if nothing goes wrong, be enough.

KIRA_CH3_STUDY.description: You lay the crown piece flat on the table and study it the way you'd study an enemy's blade, looking for the seam where it's weakest. The old texts agree on one detail that changes everything: Aethon didn't seal the Beast from the outside. He became the thing that held it, gave up the man to keep the monster contained. You trace the fragment's edge with your scarred hand and feel the cold answer back, a question with only bad options attached. Maybe there's another way that doesn't cost what his did. The texts don't offer one. You keep reading anyway, because not knowing is worse than knowing and hating it.

KIRA_CH2_LEAVE.description: You leave Tom with the Circle, telling yourself their walls and their wards will hold better than anything you could offer him on the road. They'll protect him. Train him, maybe, into something safer than what you became. You have a mother's last wish to carry and a road that's yours alone to walk now, but the weight of his face as you turned to go isn't the kind of thing that stays behind just because you did.

KIRA_CH2_COMPANION.description: The boy is standing closer than a stranger would stand, and it takes a full breath to place him under the grime and the growth spurt: Tom, the orphan from outside Millbrook, the one you pulled from a burning cellar with the void still smoking off your hands. He's found you again, or never let you go. The void lives in him too, and you'd know that cold under his skin blind. He's your student now, your responsibility in a way nothing has been since your parents. Ahead: a Temple, a mother who may not be entirely your mother, and a boy who trusts you the way you used to trust people before you learned better.

KIRA_CH3_ALONE.description: You leave the others behind at the tree line, because allies are just more people the Hollow King can use against you, and you've run out of patience for being leverage. The void inside you gets louder with every step toward the Temple, a hum behind your ribs that isn't quite sound, arriving a half-beat after you expect it. The Beast is waiting in there. So is the Hollow King. So is your mother, however much of her is left to wait for anything. Your scarred fingers have gone numb past the second knuckle, and you flex them once, testing what still answers. Whatever happens past those doors, it happens tonight.

KIRA_CH1_WATCH_TRIAL.description: Voss watches you a long moment, then tips her chin at the training yard. Three mercenaries wait, mud-caked veterans who've outlived the Waste for years by not being careless. To them you're a question that needs answering before they'll trust you with their backs. Void crackles at your fingertips, restless, and the cold of it needles under nails already scarred white from the last time you drew this deep. The Watch has fought Hollow Knights before. They know darkness when they see it walk toward them wearing a girl's face. The first mercenary charges. The second cuts left. The third holds back, reading you. Give them something worth reading.

KIRA_CH1_CIRCLE_TRIAL.description:
The Archmage leads you to a chamber where the floor doesn't agree with the tower, a void pit where reality's stitched thin enough to see the gaps. Three initiates wait, power they've had years to learn and you've had months.

'Prove your worth,' she says, and leaves you to it.

The first bolt comes as pure silence, swallowing sound on its way to your chest. You throw up a wall of shadow on instinct, frost biting your knuckles for it, and two more bolts arrive from angles you didn't have time to guess.

Your mother taught you to survive, not to fight fair. Now survive.

The initiates spread into a formation too clean to be improvised. Not a test of power. A test of who thinks faster.

choices.text: Overwhelm them with raw void power
Turn their attacks against each other
Create a barrier and wait them out

KIRA_CH1_WASTELAND_BATTLE.description: They come up out of the ash like the ground is giving birth to them: half-formed things with too many eyes and not enough flesh, drawn to the crown piece in your pocket or to whatever the void has made of your blood. Three lurch toward you first, joints bending wrong. Behind them, more rise. Six. Ten. The count keeps climbing and you stop bothering to keep it. The void answers before you ask, a cold pull settling behind your sternum, hungry already. Your mother's voice surfaces from somewhere you buried it: control the darkness, or the darkness controls you. The first creature closes the distance. You've run out of time to decide which one you're going to be tonight.

KIRA_CH1_BANDIT_AMBUSH.description:
They come from both sides of the road at once, too many to count in the time it takes to draw one breath. A dozen, not three. Word of a Nightwind on the King's Road outran you, and now every bounty hunter and Cult-paid blade in the county wants to be the one who ends you.

One wears a silver mask, moving through the others like he owns their fear. A Cult enforcer, and he's not here to fight. He's here to watch.

'Vespera sends her regards,' he says, with the particular calm of a man who has never once needed to run from anything.

The void answers before you ask it to, hot instead of cold this time, a fever climbing your arms while frost creeps backward up your sleeves toward the elbow. The merchant family lies slumped in their wagon, breathing but not waking. Bait, and you walked in wearing it.

Twelve blades. One silver mask giving the orders. And behind that mask, finally, a name that explains why the Cult never once stopped hunting you: Vespera.

choices.text: Use shadows to divide and conquer

KIRA_CH1_GUARD_POST.description:
Ten soldiers in royal colors block the road, and royal colors don't mean clean hands. Two wave a wagon through without a search, coin changing palms quick enough to be practiced. The Cult buys passage as easily as grain.

You could hand over your papers and walk through. Or show them what it costs to wear the crown's colors while serving the thing that wants it melted down.

The sergeant reads something in your face before you've decided. His hand finds his sword hilt.

'Vespera's already inside,' he sneers, stale ale on his breath. 'Too late, girl.'

Cold gathers behind your sternum, an old friend showing up uninvited. The fight starts before you've decided you wanted one.

KIRA_CH1_SHADOW_WATCH.description:
You notice them before you understand: shadows moving against the wind's grain, eyes gleaming where no eyes should be. Three Hollow Knights, corrupted mages sealed into armor never meant to breathe.

They don't speak. They just come.

Void fire streaks through the ash, your magic rising to meet it, shadow and silence colliding hard enough to crack the ground. Frost climbs your knuckles the instant you draw on it, an old debt collecting interest.

Three against one. The void wants to answer all three at once, and some cornered part of you wants to let it.

Your mother's voice surfaces from wherever you keep it buried: survive first, fight second. You've never heard her say it. You just know.

KIRA_CH1_WASTELAND_STAND.description: The creatures don't attack like animals. They come in waves, in order, like something is counting them out. You plant your feet in the ash and raise your hands, and the void answers in pillars of dark that swallow sound before they hit. The creatures drop. More climb over the fallen ones. Your fingertips have gone white at the tips, the old frostbite scars aching under fresh cold. Out past the wave-line, a Hollow Knight watches, unmoving, directing without a gesture you can see. This isn't a battle. It's a test, and someone wants to know exactly how strong the last Nightwind is.

choices.text: Push through the exhaustion—show your true power
Bargain with the void for more power

THERON_CH1_TAVERN.description:
The Broken Blade tavern reeks of stale ale and desperation. You sit in the corner, a mug of something cheap and bitter in your weathered hands. Once, men knelt before you. Once, you wore the Silver Crown of the King's Guard. Now, you wear rags and a beard grown from neglect.

The fire crackles. Other patrons avoid your table—they've heard the stories of Theron Ashford, the knight who failed his kingdom. The knight who couldn't save the king. The knight who chose survival over honor.

You drink to forget. But the void never lets you forget. It whispers in your dreams, in thebottom of your cup, in the eyes of every noble you've learned to hate.

The tavern door creaks open. A woman enters, cloaked in midnight blue. Her eyes find you immediately.

'Theron Ashford,' she says. 'I've been looking for you.'

choices.text: Ignore her—another creditor or bounty hunter
Fight—show her you're still a knight!

THERON_CH1_IGNORE.description:
You don't look up. Can't she see you're busy drowning your sorrows? The void stirs in your chest—that familiar ache of power you no longer deserve.

She sits across from you anyway. Her hand removes her hood, revealing silver-streaked hair and a face carved by decades of court intrigue. This is no ordinary hunter.

'I am Lady Morgana Vance,' she says. 'High Advisor to Queen Seraphina. And I've come to offer you something precious: redemption.'

The word hits like a blade. Redemption. You almost laugh.

'Your queen's guard killed my honor,' you say. 'Your queen's throne is built on lies.'

Morgana smiles coldly. 'Then help me unbuild it. There's a Cult that operates in her name. The Ashfield Cult. And they need to be stopped.'

choices.text: Ask for time to think—too many scars

THERON_CH1_DEMAND.description:
Your hand moves to your belt—where your sword used to hang. You sold it three months ago for drink money. But the reflex remains.

The woman's eyes flick to your movement, then back to your face. No fear. Interesting.

'I am Lady Morgana Vance,' she says. 'High Advisor to Queen Seraphina. And I don't have time for your posturing, Theron. Your king is dead. Your order is scattered. And your drinking won't bring them back.'

The words cut deeper than any blade. You could kill her. No—you could never. But the rage is familiar. Better than the emptiness.

'What do you want?' you growl.

She slides a parchment across the table. 'The Ashfield Cult. They've infiltrated the court. I need a hunter, not a knight. Someone who understands darkness.'

Her eyes meet yours. 'Someone like you.'

choices.text: Negotiate terms—nothing free in this world

THERON_CH1_DECLINE.description:
You shake your head, not even looking at her. 'I'm retired. Find another broken knight to do your bidding.'

But she doesn't leave. Instead, she sits uninvited, her presence filling the space like a storm cloud.

'Your princess died in that fire, Theron.'

The room goes silent. The void inside you roars.

'Princess Elara. Trapped in the burning palace while you escaped. The king ordered you to save her, and you chose yourself.'

Your hand shakes around the mug. 'I tried—'

'You tried to save yourself. The princess burned. And the Cult that set that fire? They're still out there. Worshipping false gods. Building power in the shadows.'

She leans close. 'Don't you want to know why?'

The question hangs in the air. And for the first time in years, you feel something other than emptiness. You feel hate.

choices.text: Who is she really?—dig for truth
Walk away—some debts can't be paid

THERON_CH1_MISSION.description:
You stand—your knees aching, your back sore from years of hunched drinking. But for the first time, something feels right.

'告诉我,' you say. 'Everything.'

Morgana outlines the mission: The Ashfield Cult worships the Void—that ancient nothing that existed before creation. They've been secretly sacrificing nobles, stealing artifacts, building toward something called the Convergence.

'Vespera,' Morgana says. 'Their leader. Once a priestess of the light, now something else. She holds court in the old temple outside Thornwick.'

She hands you a purse—heavy with gold. 'Find them. Expose them. And if you can... bring me proof of their conspiracy.'

You take the gold. You take the name. And you take the first step toward either redemption or ruin.

THERON_CH1_PAYMENT.description:
You hold out your hand. 'Gold. Fifty sovereigns. Upfront.'

Morgana raises an eyebrow. 'You were once a knight of the realm. Now you bargain like a mercenary?'

'I was a knight of a dead king. Now I'm just a man who needs drink money.'

She reaches into her cloak and produces a pouch. It clinks with more than fifty coins.

'Seventy,' she says. 'But this comes with strings. I need the Cult exposed—not just destroyed. Evidence that will convince the court. The queen's council is compromised, Theron. I need proof, not bodies.'

You weigh the pouch. Seventy sovereigns. Enough to drink yourself to death twice over.

'Or,' she adds, 'I could simply leave. Find another broken knight. There's always more.'

The challenge hangs in the air. You take the gold.

choices.text: Accept but with conditions—you won't kill innocents

THERON_CH1_HESITATE.description:
You don't answer. Can't she see the scars? The memories? Every time you close your eyes, you see the flames. The princess screaming your name. The king's body, broken and burned.

'I'll be here tomorrow,' Morgana says. 'And the day after. The Cult won't wait forever. Neither will I.'

She leaves a card on the table—an address in the lower city. Then she's gone, swallowed by the night.

You stare at the card for hours. Redemption. Such a small word for such an enormous lie.

But dawn finds you outside that address. Old habits die hard. Duty, even when it's murdered, sometimes leaves a pulse.

choices.text: Meet her at dawn—you've made your choice

THERON_CH1_SUSPICION.description:
You hold her gaze. 'Why me? Any knight could do this. Any spy. Any assassin.'

Morgana's smile is thin. 'Because any of them might be bought. The Cult has gold, Theron. More gold than the crown itself. But you?'

She leans back. 'You have nothing left to buy. And everything to prove.'

That stings. But it's also true.

'I'll check your story,' you say. 'If you're lying, I'll find out.'

She nods. 'I would expect nothing less. The Ashfield Cult meets in the old temple at Thornwick. Their leader goes by Vespera. That's all I know—and all I need you to discover.'

She stands to leave. 'I'll be in touch.'

But as the door closes, you notice something: her signet ring. It bears a symbol—a crescent moon, intertwined with shadows. The symbol of the old priesthood.

The same symbol, you realize, that's been carved into the bodies of the Cult's victims.

choices.text: Reject her entirely—she's part of it

THERON_CH1_LEAVE.description:
You stand abruptly, the chair scraping against the floor. 'Find someone else. I don't do politics. I don't do redemption. I don't do queens.'

Morgana's voice follows you to the door. 'The princess knew your name, Theron. She screamed it. And you left her to burn.'

You pause at the threshold. The void roars inside you, demanding silence.

'You're already dead,' she calls. 'A man walking around in a corpse. That's not living—that's hiding. And we both know it.'

You walk into the night. The cold air hits your face, and for a moment, you feel nothing at all.

But three blocks later, you're stealing a sword from a drunk guard. And heading toward Thornwick.

Maybe death is better than this emptiness. Maybe this mission is just a complicated way to end it.

choices.text: Look for another lead—this feels wrong

THERON_CH1_DELAY.description:
One more day. That's all you ask. One more day to drink, to pretend, to ignore the call of the void.

But the void doesn't wait. It never waits.

That night, you dream of flames. Princess Elara stands in the center of a burning room, her hands raised, calling for you. You reach for her—and your hands pass through like smoke.

She burns. And you watch.

Dawn brings sickness, not answers. Your hands shake. Your head pounds. And worst of all: the dream won't fade.

You find Morgana's address. But something has changed. You're not doing this for redemption anymore. You're doing it because if you stop, you'll drown in the flames.

choices.text: Tell her about the dreams—she knows something

THERON_CH1_PREPARE.description:
You spend the night preparing—not for redemption, but for war. A sword from a dead guard. A dagger hidden in your boot. Bread and dried meat for the road.

The void responds to your preparation. It whispers of weaknesses, of angles of attack, of where to strike. You'd forgotten how much you knew.

In the morning, you feel almost human. Almost ready.

But Morgana isn't at her address. Instead, a messenger waits with a note: 'Temple of the Ash. Midnight. Come alone.'

The Temple of the Ash is a ruin—a burnt-out church from the old king's time. No one goes there. No one remembers why.

Except you do. That's where the fire started. That's where the Cult made their first sacrifice.

choices.text: Go to the temple—confront her there

THERON_CH1_CONFRONT.description:
You turn back, your hand on the door. 'Your ring. The crescent moon and shadow.'

Morgana freezes. For a moment, something flashes in her eyes—fear?

'The old priesthood,' you continue. 'They served the Void before the kingdom was born. And that symbol—it matches the Cult's marks.'

She slowly turns, her hand going to the ring. 'There are two kinds of people in this world, Theron. Those who serve the void, and those who use it against itself.'

She removes the ring, placing it on the table. 'I was young when they took me. A priestess of the old ways. But I found the light instead—the queen's light. And I've spent thirty years fighting what I was.'

Her eyes meet yours. 'Now do you understand? They want me dead. They've tried before. I need someone who knows their darkness.'

THERON_CH1_JOURNEY.description:
The road to Thornwick winds through the Ashen Hills—a territory that hasn't recovered from the old wars. Burnt trees dot the landscape like skeletal fingers reaching for a sky that never lightens.

You travel for three days. The void keeps you company—it whispers of danger ahead, of traps and treachery. You stopped questioning it years ago.

On the second night, you make camp near an abandoned village. The houses are blackened, their occupants long since fled or taken. The Cult did this, you know. Their work.

You find a survivor hiding in a cellar—an old woman, half-mad with fear.

'They took the children,' she whispers. 'All of them. For the Convergence. The shadow lady will be so pleased.'

The shadow lady. Vespera.

She dies in your arms before you can learn more. But the word stays with you: Convergence. Something big is coming.

choices.text: Set a trap—let them come to you

THERON_CH1_ALLIES.description:
You don't go to Thornwick alone. Old contacts remain—mercenaries, spies, other broken knights who owe you favors from a life you try to forget.

There's Garreth, a former guard captain who lost his commission for drinking. Brynn, a thief who once tried to pick your pocket and ended up owing you her life. Old Marcus, a healer with no love for the queen but deep hatred for the Cult.

They gather in a seedy tavern in the lower city, their faces carved by years of survival. They look at you with something like hope.

'The Ashfield Cult,' you tell them. 'We've heard stories. Bad ones.' Brynn shakes her head. 'They take children, Theron. For rituals. For sacrifices.'

Garreth spits on the floor. 'The queen's court is compromised. Everyone knows it. But no one can prove it.'

You have your team. Now you need a plan.

THERON_CH1_RESEARCH.description:
You spend two days in the great library of Thornwick—or what's left of it. The Cult burned most of the records, but some survived.

The Ashfield Cult emerged thirty years ago, following the old king's death. They worship the Void—that primordial darkness before creation. Their goal: the Convergence, when all shadows merge into one, ending the world as we know it.

Their leader, Vespera, was once a priestess of the Light. She was beautiful once—a saint, they called her. But something broke her. Or perhaps she was always broken, waiting to be unleashed.

The records mention artifacts: seven shards of the Void Crown, scattered after the Sundering. The Cult has six. The seventh is hidden.

And when they reunite all seven shards... the Convergence will begin.

You close the last book. The world is stranger than you knew. And darker.

choices.text: Find the seventh shard—stop the Convergence
Contact the Circle of Mages—seek help

THERON_CH1_AMBUSH.description:
The road through the hills narrows into a canyon. Bad territory. You should know better—once, you were a knight who understood tactics.

But the void doesn't warn you. It wants this.

Arrows strike from the rocks above. You twist, one piercing your shoulder, but the others miss. You draw your stolen sword and charge the shadows.

Cultists drop from the rocks—six of them, blades gleaming with poison. Their eyes are empty, their faces flat. They've given themselves to the void. There's no reasoning with them.

You fight with forgotten skill—the training of a lifetime muscle memory. The void responds, lending you strength you thought was gone. The first two fall. Then the third. But more keep coming.

An arrow takes you in the leg. You go down. And the Cultists circle, ready to finish it.

choices.text: Fight to the death—don't give up
Use the void to escape—darkness shields you

THERON_CH1_SURVIVORS.description:
You search the ruins for two days. Most are dead—but a few survived, hiding in basements and crawlspaces.

A young boy, perhaps ten, is the only one who can speak. The Cult took his sister. 'The shadow lady wants her for the big ceremony,' he says. 'She said when the moons align, the darkness will come.'

You learn more: The Cult holds their base in the old temple, three miles north. They have dozens of followers, and more arriving every day. And they're preparing for something called the Night of Convergence.

The boy begs to come with you. You should leave him—he'll only slow you down. But his eyes remind you of someone. Princess Elara, perhaps. Another child you couldn't save.

'Stay hidden,' you tell him. 'I'll bring your sister back.'

The promise feels like ash in your mouth. But it's the only thing you have left to give.

choices.text: Take him with you—he deserves justice

THERON_CH1_TRAP.description:
You set a trap—a false trail leading to an abandoned mill. Cultists will follow, thinking you isolated. Instead, they'll walk into a killzone.

It takes hours to prepare, but the void helps. It always helps when death is involved.

Three Cultists take the bait. They're careless, expecting an easy target. They find you instead.

The fight is short. Your blade takes the first through the throat. The second falls to your counter. The third tries to run—you let him go, so he'll bring others.

But something feels wrong. The Cultists are too weak, too poorly trained. They fight like fanatics, not soldiers.

A noise behind you. You spin—but it's already too late. A blade pierces your side.

Vespera steps from the shadows. She's beautiful, in a terrible way. Her eyes hold the void itself.

'Theron Ashford,' she says. 'I've been waiting for you.'

THERON_CH1_SURVIVE.description:
You fight like the knight you once were. The void runs through your veins, lending strength to your blade. Blood paints the rocks. Your wounds don't matter. Nothing matters but survival.

Five Cultists fall. Then ten. The arrow in your shoulder tears as you move, but the pain is distant, irrelevant. You've entered the void-state—pure combat, pure instinct.

When it's over, you stand alone in the canyon, covered in blood. Yours or theirs—you don't know. The void recedes, leaving exhaustion in its wake.

Your injuries are serious. Without help, you won't survive the journey. But you've won. For now.

A survivor among the Cultists—a young woman, barely more than a girl—lies dying against a rock. She looks at you with empty eyes.

'The Convergence is coming,' she gasps. 'Nothing can stop it. Nothing.'

She dies. But her words echo in your mind.

choices.text: Return to Morgana—report what you know

THERON_CH1_ESCAPE.description:
The void wraps around you like a cloak. Shadow and silence—your old friends. You've forgotten how to use them, but they remember you.

You become darkness, slipping between the Cultists. Their blades pass through where you stood. Their arrows miss by inches. You flow like smoke, untouchable.

Seconds later, you're at the temple entrance. The Cultists are still searching, confused.

Inside, the temple is vast—a cathedral of shadows. The altar at its heart glows with sickly light. And there, suspended above it: children. Dozens of them. Sacrifices waiting to be made.

The void screams at you to act. But first, you need to understand. What is the Convergence? What does Vespera really want?

You find a hidden vantage point. And watch.

THERON_CH1_CAPTURE.description:
You let them take you. It's not surrender—it's strategy. They bind your hands with iron chains blessed by priests, but they don't know about the void inside you. It recognizes the chains for what they are: inconveniences.

They drag you to the temple. It's larger than you imagined—a sprawling complex built over centuries, layer upon layer of worship and sacrifice. They don't just worship the void here. They study it.

The children are held in the lower chambers. Dozens of them, drugged into sleep. Their souls will feed the Convergence.

But you see something else: maps. Charts. The Cult's full plan.

The Night of Convergence is three days away. When the moons align, the seven shards will merge, tearing open a portal to the void itself. And through that portal, something ancient will emerge.

The Void Beast. A god that was sealed away at the Sundering. And now, it's waking up.

choices.text: Use the void to contact Morgana

THERON_CH2_SHARD.description:
The research leads you to a crypt beneath the old cathedral. The seventh shard has been hidden here for thirty years—a safeguard against the very convergence the Cult now pursues.

The chamber is sealed with void wards. Only someone who walks the darkness can enter. Only someone with nothing left to lose can claim the power.

Inside, the shard floats in a sphere of shadow. It's beautiful and terrible—a piece of something that should never exist.

When you touch it, visions flood your mind. The Void Beast, rising from its prison. The world ending in shadow. And at the center of it all: you, transformed into something beyond human.

The shard calls to you. It wants to be used. It wants you to become the Convergence's instrument—or its weapon against the Cult.

You take the shard. Power floods through you—darkness made manifest. And somewhere, across the kingdom, Vespera feels your presence.

choices.text: Use the shard against the Cult
Hide the shard—it cannot be used
Bring it to Morgana—trust the queen

THERON_CH2_CIRCLE.description:
The Circle of Mages hides in plain sight—a tower that appears different to each observer. You climb the stairs, each step taking you further from the world you knew.

The Archmage awaits at the top. An ageless woman with silver hair and eyes that see through time.

'Theron Ashford,' she says. 'We wondered when you'd come.'

She knows about the Cult. About Vespera. About the Convergence.

'The Void Beast was sealed at the Sundering,' she explains. 'Seven shards of its power were scattered across the kingdoms. The Cult has collected six. If they claim the seventh...'

She doesn't finish. She doesn't have to.

'We can help you,' she offers. 'But the price is your service. Become our weapon against the darkness, and we'll help you destroy the Cult.'

THERON_CH2_TEMPLE.description:
The Temple of Ash rises against the grey sky—a monolith of black stone that's stood for a thousand years. This is where the first Convergence was attempted. This is where it will happen again.

You approach through the outer defenses. Cultists scatter before you—either fleeing or attacking. You cut through them without mercy. The void has made you strong.

Inside, the temple is a maze. Chambers upon chambers, each dedicated to a different aspect of the void. Sacrifice rooms. Meditation halls. Torture chambers where the faithful are broken and remade.

You find the main chamber—the heart of the temple. Seven pedestals ring a central dais. Six hold shards of terrible power, glowing with barely contained darkness. The seventh is empty.

At the dais stands a woman. Vespera. She turns, and you see her face.

And you realize you know her.

THERON_CH2_VESPERA.description:
Vespera's face is beautiful and terrible. You know those features—softened by decades, but unmistakable.

'Princess Elara?'

She smiles. It's wrong. Too many teeth. Too much darkness.

'Brother Theron. I wondered when you'd come. I've been waiting for you.'

Princess Elara—the child you couldn't save—is now the leader of the Cult. The fire didn't kill her. It transformed her.

'I burned that night,' she says. 'I burned, and I died, and I was reborn in the void. The Beast spoke to me. It showed me the truth. The world is a wound, Theron. And the void will heal it.'

She reaches for you. 'Join me. Together, we can complete the Convergence. We can end all the pain. All the suffering. All the memories that keep you awake at night.'

Your hand tightens on your sword. But she's not done.

'Or kill me,' she says. 'But then the Convergence happens anyway. I'm not the key—the shards are. And I have six of them.'

choices.text: Talk her out of it—use the past

THERON_CH2_VESPERA_TRAP.description:
You lower your blade, pretending to consider her offer. Your eyes scan the chamber—the six shards glowing on their pedestals, the children suspended above the altar, the portal already opening in the center.

This is a trap. You know it. She knows it.

'How do I know I can trust you?' you ask, buying time.

Elara laughs—the sound is wrong, like glass breaking. 'You can't. But you have no other choice. Join me, or watch the world burn.'

The portal pulses. The Beast stirs. And you realize: you have maybe five minutes before the Convergence completes. Enough time to act—but only if you're clever.

'I'll need the seventh shard,' you say. 'I'll bring it to you—but let the children go first.'

She tilts her head, considering. Then nods.

The first children are released. One minute. Two. You're running out of time.

THERON_CH3_WAIT.description:
You wait. Hours pass. The Cult grows desperate—they attack villages, sacrifice children, unleash chaos. The kingdom cries out for a hero.

But you wait.

The Circle sends hunters. Morgana demands answers. Even Vespera sends messages: 'I know you have the seventh. Meet me, or more children die.'

Still you wait.

And then, on the night of the Convergence, you move. The Cult has gathered all their forces at the temple—all six shards glowing on their altar. Vespera stands ready to complete the ritual.

But she's missing one thing.

You.

With the seventh shard, you can either complete the Convergence yourself—or destroy it forever. The choice, finally, is yours.

choices.text: Claim the power but keep it contained

THERON_CH2_MESSENGER.description:
You find a crossroads rider heading toward Crownhaven. For ten gold, he agrees to carry your message to Lady Morgana Vance.

The boy watches you, worried. 'Will she come? Will she save my sister?'

You don't answer. You don't know. Morgana has her own agenda, and the boy is just another piece on a board he doesn't understand.

Days pass. The boy grows impatient. Then, on the third night, a rider appears at the edge of the village—not Morgana, but a messenger bearing her seal.

'She's coming,' the messenger says. 'But she's not alone. The queen has sent the Guard. This isn't a rescue mission anymore. It's a war.'

choices.text: Prepare for war—train the boy to fight
Go to meet Morgana—coordinate the attack
Keep the boy hidden—war is no place for children

THERON_CH2_COMPANION.description:
The boy, Caden, follows you through the hills. He doesn't speak much—too much trauma—but he moves quickly, staying close.

You make camp the first night under an overpass. He watches you sharpen your sword, then speaks.

'My sister—she has the void in her. She told me. She's scared, but she said it doesn't hurt anymore.'

You pause. The void in a child. That changes everything.

'What was her name?'

'Lily. She was taken three days ago. The shadow lady wanted her for something special.'

The boy looks at you with hope. 'You said you'd bring her back. Is that still true?'

You think about Princess Elara. About the fire. About all the things you've failed to save.

'I'll try,' you say. And for the first time in years, you mean it.

choices.text: Find a safe place—wait for the right moment

THERON_CH2_TRAINING.description:
The Circle of Mages offers training—a way to master the void without being consumed. You accept, knowing it's probably a trap. But you need power.

Days pass in isolation. The Archmage teaches you to feel the void—and to control it. Not to deny your darkness, but to shape it.

You learn to draw shadows like weapons. To step through darkness like doors. To speak in the language of nothing.

But during your training, you discover a secret: The Circle isn't noble. They've been manipulating the kingdom for centuries. The king didn't die in a fire—they killed him.

And the queen? She's their puppet.

Everything is a lie. The Cult. The Court. The Circle. Everyone serves the void in their own way.

But you also learn this: The seventh shard is hidden in the temple itself. The original seal, placed to prevent exactly this.

choices.text: Expose the Circle—their plan must end
Leave both sides—let them destroy each other

THERON_CH2_HEALER.description:
A healer lives in the hills—an old woman who remembers the old kingdom. She tends your wounds in silence, her hands glowing with light magic.

'You carry the void,' she says. It's not a question.

You don't deny it. 'It won't let me die. I've tried.'

She laughs—a dry, humorless sound. 'It won't let you live either. It's waiting, Theron. Feeding on your pain. The day you stop suffering is the day it consumes you.'

She speaks of a solution: The Temple of Ash holds the only seal strong enough to contain the void permanently. An ancient binding, placed during the Sundering.

'But it's a prison,' she warns. 'Once you enter, you cannot leave. The void will be sealed inside you forever.'

Is that death? Or is it peace?

choices.text: Go to the temple—accept the seal
Find another way—there has to be one

THERON_CH3_BATTLE.description:
You charge across the chamber, your blade aimed at Vespera's heart. She doesn't move—doesn't even flinches.

Her hand catches your sword. The blade stops an inch from her chest.

'Such anger,' she whispers. 'Is it for me? Or for yourself?'

Shadows wrap around you. Not attacking—embracing. The void recognizes its own.

'You can't kill me, Theron. I'm part of you now. I've always been part of you—the part you left in that burning palace.'

She releases you. You stagger back, weapon raised.

'The Convergence will happen. One way or another. But you have a choice: be destroyed by it, or become its instrument.'

Around you, the six shards begin to glow. The walls shake. The moors align.

The ritual is beginning.

choices.text: Claim the power—become the new master

THERON_CH3_TALK.description:
You lower your blade. Not surrender—strategy.

'Elara,' you say. 'Remember that night? The fire? You reached for me. I couldn't reach back.'

Her eye twitches. The darkness wavers.

'I've spent years running,' you continue. 'Trying to forget. Drinking to silence the voices. But they're not silenced. They just get louder.'

You step closer. 'Let me help you. We can end this together—properly. No more sacrifices. No more children.'

She hesitates. For a moment, you see the girl she used to be—sweet, innocent, full of light.

'There's no proper way,' she whispers. 'The void is here. It's always been here. It's in our blood—our family line.'

Your blood. The Ashford line. You've carried the void since birth.

'Then we carry it together,' you say. 'But choose for yourself. Choose to end it.'

choices.text: Help her destroy the shards—end the line
Kill her anyway—she's too far gone

THERON_CH3_JOIN.description:
You kneel. The void rises to greet you—a wave of darkness that feels like coming home.

Elara touches your head, her fingers cold as ice.

'Welcome home, brother. We've been waiting for you.'

The power floods through you—seven shards merged into one. You feel the seal breaking. The Beast stirring.

And you understand what you've become: the Vessel. The new prison for the Void Beast. The door that's about to open.

'Is this what you wanted?' you ask.

'No,' she says. 'This is what we needed. The old gods must have a host. And you're stronger than any cultist.'

The convergence begins. Not with fire and destruction—but with silence. The void consumes everything. Your mind. Your memories. Your soul.

But in the end, you control it. You become its master.

And that's when you realize: you were never the hero. You were always the monster. You just didn't know it yet.

THERON_CH3_RITUAL.description:
You watch from the shadows as Vespera begins the ritual.

The six shards rise from their pedestals, circling the dais. She stands at the center, her arms raised, her voice chanting in a language older than words.

The children wake—dozens of them. Their eyes glow with void light. They're not victims—they're components. Fuel for the machine.

The portal opens slowly—a tear in reality itself. Beyond it, you see the Beast. A darkness beyond darkness. A hunger beyond hunger.

It looks at you.

It sees you.

'Join us,' it whispers. 'You've always been ours.'

You could run. Let it consume the world. But then you remember Princess Elara—the child she used to be. The sister you couldn't save.

No more.

choices.text: Claim the portal—become the Beast's voice

THERON_CH3_SEAL.description:
The seal is ancient—a binding placed during the Sundering to contain the Void Beast forever. It sits at the heart of the temple, waiting.

You touch the stone. Visions flood your mind.

The Sundering. The original convergence. Countless deaths to seal away a god. And the knights who made that sacrifice—your ancestors.

You've been running from this your whole life. It was always your destiny.

The void inside you responds to the seal. It wants in. It wants rest.

You step into the chamber, and the seal closes around you. The darkness floods into you—concentrated, overwhelming. Your body becomes the prison. Your soul becomes the lock.

You scream—but the scream becomes silence. The void becomes you.

And in that eternal moment, you feel peace. The first peace you've known since the fire.

They'll never escape. Neither will you.

choices.text: Accept the seal—you are the prison now
Find release—in the void you are one

THERON_CH3_CONFRONTATION.description:
The seventh shard burns in your hand. You've drawn upon power that should never exist—and you've made your choice.

Vespera sees you across the chamber. Her eyes widen.

'You got the seventh,' she breathes. 'How?'

'I am the seventh,' you say. 'I've always been. Since the fire. Since Elara died. Since I became darkness itself.'

The shard responds to your will. You could destroy the Cult. All of them. The entire temple. Everything.

But that would make you the monster they've been fighting.

Instead, you focus the power into the seal—the ancient binding that held the Beast once. It won't hold forever, but it will hold.

'No,' Vespera screams. 'Don't—'

The seal activates. The shards shatter. The portal collapses.

And you're left standing in the ruins, holding nothing.

The Beast is sealed. The Cult is scattered. And you?

You're empty.

THERON_CH3_EXPOSE.description:
The truth is a weapon. And you've just loaded the cannon.

You return to Crownhaven with proof: documents, witnesses, the testimony of a dozen survivors. The Circle's conspiracy. The Cult's infiltration. Morgana's true allegiance.

But things have changed while you were gone. Queen Seraphina is dead. Assassinated. And in her place sits a new ruler—someone you've never seen.

The court is divided. The kingdom is on the brink of civil war.

'Vespera demanded this,' Morgana says. 'The Cult wanted chaos. They needed the queen dead to begin the Convergence.'

But it's too late. The shards are aligning. The portal is opening.

You have one choice left: Use what you've learned to stop the Convergence, or use it to claim power.

The throne sits empty. And you have the strength to take it.

THERON_CH3_ALONE.description:
You leave both sides to destroy each other. The Circle. The Cult. The Court. Let them tear each other apart.

But the world doesn't end. Hours pass, then days. The portal doesn't open. The Beast doesn't rise.

Something has changed. Vespera is dead—killed by her own followers. The shards have scattered. The Convergence has failed.

You wander the kingdom, aimless. The void inside you has quieted—or perhaps it's just sleeping.

You find yourself at the broken tavern where this all began. The mug is still there, waiting.

Youpour it out.

For the first time in years, you don't reach for the drink. There's no peace in it. No forgetting. Only more dreams of flames.

But perhaps there's something else. Perhaps there's the beginning of a different path.

You leave the tavern. The road stretches ahead. And for the first time in years, you don't know where it leads.

That's okay. You'll figure it out as you go.

THERON_ENDING_REDEEMED.description:
You sacrifice yourself to save the world. It's the only noble thing you've ever done.

The void consumes you—or perhaps you become it. Either way, the Beast is sealed. The Cult is destroyed. The children are saved.

In the final moment, you see Princess Elara—not the monster she became, but the child she was. She's reaching for you. And this time, you reach back.

'Thank you,' she whispers. 'For not giving up on me.'

You smile. 'I never gave up on myself. I just didn't know it yet.'

The darkness takes you. But it's not empty. It's not suffering. It's rest.

The kingdom calls you a hero. They build statues. They write songs. But you don't hear them. You're beyond their praise, beyond their blame.

In the void, you watch. And wait. And protect.

Perhaps one day, you'll be called again. And perhaps next time, you'll be ready.

THERON_ENDING_COMMANDER.description:
You claim power. It's what you've always been, perhaps—the monster in the shadows, waiting to be unleashed.

With the void at your command, you become something more than a knight. You become a ruler. The kingdom kneels to you—not because they want to, but because they have no choice.

The Cult is scattered. The Circle is broken. The court is your tool. And you sit upon the throne, looking down at a world that fears you.

Is this redemption? No. It's something else. Something darker.

But you rule with a firm hand. No chaos. No Cults. No dangerous magic. The kingdom is safe—so safe it chills.

And sometimes, late at night, when you're alone in the throne room, you feel the void moving. It wants more. It always wants more.

Perhaps one day, you'll lose yourself entirely. Become the very thing you fought. But not today.

Today, you rule. And that's enough.

THERON_ENDING_HOLLOW.description:
You survive. That's all. You always said you would.

The world burns around you. The Cult rises. The Circle falls. The kingdom descends into chaos. And you walk away, leaving nothing but footprints in the ash.

The void lives inside you—patient, hungry, waiting. You've learned to feed it just enough to keep it quiet. Other people's pain. Other people's sacrifices. Other people's children.

You don't dream anymore. The flames have stopped. Elara's voice has faded. But something else lives in your sleep: a vast darkness, endless and cold. It speaks in a language you almost understand.

'Not yet,' it whispers. 'But soon.'

You wander the roads, looking for something you'll never find. A purpose. A peace. An end.

But the void doesn't end. It just waits.

And one day, when you've finally lost everything else, when there's nothing left to feed on but your own soul, it'll be waiting.

The Hollow Knight walks forever. And the void is his kingdom.

THERON_ENDING_SURVIVOR.description:
You survive. That's all you've ever done.

The battle ends without you. The Cult falls—scattered, broken, their ritual incomplete. The Void Beast remains sealed. And you? You walk away, leaving nothing but footprints in the ash.

No one calls you a hero. No songs are sung in your name. You didn't save the kingdom or destroy the evil. You just lived—one more day, then another, then another.

The void stays inside you. Sometimes it's quiet. Sometimes it screams. But you've learned to feed it just enough to keep it calm. Other people's pain. Other people's sacrifices.

You wander the roads, passing through villages that don't know your face, drinking in taverns where no one remembers your name. The knight who failed. The hero who ran.

But somewhere, in the deepest part of your soul, you know you made the right choice. The world didn't need another monster. It just needed someone to survive.

And you did. You always will.

The Survivor walks on. And the void walks with him.

THERON_ENDING_KING.description:
You take the throne. Not through birthright or marriage, but through power—the void itself bends to your will.

The Cult kneels to you. The Circle obeys. Even Morgana bows, though her eyes hold fear. The kingdom is yours—every stone, every soul, every shadow.

You rule with the void at your command. No one dares rebel. No one can.

But the throne is cold. Lonely. You sit alone in the throne room, looking down at a world that fears you. Is this what you wanted? Is this what Elara wanted?

Sometimes, late at night, you feel the Beast stirring. It wants more. It always wants more. And you realize: you're not the master. You're the Vessel. The cage that holds the darkness.

One day, the cage will break. The Beast will escape. And everything you've built will fall.

But not today. Today, you rule. And that's enough.

The Void King sits upon his throne. And waits.

THERON_ENDING_BETRAYED.description:
In the end, you are betrayed by those you trusted most. The kingdom turns against you, calling you a traitor, a monster, a servant of shadow.

You fade into legend - a cautionary tale told to children. The knight who fell from grace and never rose again.

But in the darkness, you live on. Watching. Waiting. And perhaps, one day, the truth will emerge.

THERON_CH1_DREAMS.description:
The fever takes you in the night. Your body burns while your mind wanders through memories you thought you'd buried.

You see the palace burning. Princess Elara at the window, reaching for you. You see the king falling, his Silver Crown clattering on stone. You see the Cultists emerging from the smoke, their blades already wet.

And then you see something new: a shadow woman standing over a circle of children, chanting in a language older than words. She's beautiful and terrible, and when she looks at you through the dream, she smiles.

'I know you,' she whispers. 'I've always known you. We share the same blood.'

You wake gasping, sweat-soaked, the void screaming inside your chest. Dawn light filters through cracked shutters. Your hands won't stop shaking.

choices.text: Find Morgana immediately—share what you saw
Research the bloodline—who is she really?

THERON_CH1_TEMPLE.description:
The Temple of Ash is exactly as Morgana described—a burnt husk of old faith, its spires blackened by flames that never fully went out. The locals call it cursed. They're not wrong.

You enter at midnight, your stolen sword drawn. The void guides your steps, pulling you deeper into the darkness. The temple is layered: what you see above ground is nothing compared to the catacombs below.

In the main chamber, you find symbols carved into the floor—the same crescent moon and shadow that marked Morgana's ring. And there, burned into the stone as if by acid: a message.

'Welcome home, brother. We've been waiting.'

The temperature drops. Behind you, footsteps echo.

choices.text: Turn and face whoever's behind you
Call out—demand to know who's there

THERON_CH1_ASSAULT.description:
The temple's outer walls fall in minutes. Your allies break through the courtyard, killing guards, clearing the way. For the first time in years, you lead men into battle. And it feels right.

Inside, the temple is a labyrinth. Your group splits, covering more ground. You push toward the inner sanctum where, according to your research, the Cult's leadership gathers.

A hooded figure blocks your path. She doesn't raise a weapon—she doesn't need to. The void radiates from her like heat from a forge.

'Stop,' she says. 'This isn't what you want.'

But it is what you want. This ends tonight.

choices.text: Order your allies to flank while you distract

THERON_CH2_BATTLE.description:
The inner chamber erupts into chaos. Cultists pour from hidden passages, their eyes glowing with void light. Your allies fight back-to-back, steel ringing against steel.

But something is wrong. They're not trying to kill you—they're herding you. Leading you somewhere specific.

The void screams warning, but it's too late. The floor gives way beneath your feet. You fall into darkness, landing hard on cold stone.

When you look up, you see the altar room—and the six shards hovering above it, pulsing with terrible power. And standing at the center, hands raised in welcome: Vespera.

'The last Ashford,' she says. 'The seventh shard. We finally meet.'

choices.text: Claim a shard—turn their power against them
Talk to her—she called you family

THERON_CH2_MESSAGE.description:
The void responds to your need. It's been waiting to be used—and this is exactly what it was made for. Shadows gather around your bound hands, twisting into shapes that carry your thoughts across miles.

You send everything to Morgana: the temple's location, the number of Cultists, the children's location, and the truth about Vespera. Your message arrives like a knife in the night.

But the effort costs you. The void feeds on your pain, your memories, your humanity. When the message leaves, you feel emptier than before.

Hours later, the door creaks open. A Cultist throws water on your face. 'The Master wants to see you.'

Your message was received. Now the real test begins.

choices.text: Fight your way out—now you have backup coming

THERON_CH2_PRISON.description:
The Cultists throw you into a cell carved from black stone. No chains this time—they're confident you can't escape. They're right. The walls are void-touched, designed to drain magic.

But they don't know about your connection. The void is part of you, and it reaches through the walls, feeding on the temple's own darkness.

Days blur together. Food comes. Water comes. And occasionally, a child is brought to your cell—not for you to hurt, but for you to teach.

'You're like us,' one of them whispers. 'The shadow knows you.'

You teach them what you can. How to resist. How to survive. How to hold onto light in absolute darkness.

And you wait. Morgana will come. The void promises it.

choices.text: Plan an escape with the children
Use void magic to contact someone outside

THERON_CH2_REPORT.description:
Morgana receives you in her private chamber. You lay out everything: the temple's layout, the Cult's numbers, the ritual schedule, and most importantly—the identity of Vespera.

'Princess Elara,' Morgana repeats, her face pale. 'That's... that's impossible. She died in the fire.'

'Not according to the Cult. She was transformed by the void. Made into something new. And she wants me for the Convergence.'

Morgana is silent for a long moment. 'The queen will want to handle this diplomatically. Send envoys. Negotiate.'
